user_query:
I’m preparing a simple cost dashboard and a brief stakeholder update. Using the provided local data and config, please:

1) Financial analysis and CSV output
- Read data/transactions.csv and data/budget.csv.
- Compute Q1 2024 totals by category: Actual (sum of transaction amounts in Jan–Mar 2024), Budget (sum of monthly budgets for the same period), and Variance = Actual - Budget.
- Output the results to output/q1_category_variance.csv with columns: category,actual,budget,variance. Use two decimals for all amounts. Sort rows by descending absolute variance so the largest variance category appears first.
- Do not hardcode values; derive them from the CSV inputs.

2) Chart configuration update (ECharts-style JSON)
- Modify src/chartConfig.json in place to reflect the computed category order and values from step 1.
- Set aria.enabled to true.
- Set color to ["#2E7D32", "#1565C0"] (Actual = green, Budget = blue).
- Set tooltip.valueFormatter to the exact string "$,.2f".
- Set xAxis.data to the category names in the same order as output/q1_category_variance.csv.
- Set series[0].name to "Actual" and series[0].data to the Actual amounts aligned with xAxis.data.
- Set series[1].name to "Budget" and series[1].data to the Budget amounts aligned with xAxis.data.
- Leave all other existing fields unchanged.

3) Rewrite stakeholder message for tone and clarity
- Read docs/stakeholder_draft.md and rewrite it into output/stakeholder_update.md.
- Requirements for the rewrite:
  - Keep it under 120 words, calm and constructive, no blamey tone.
  - Include the total Q1 Actual spend and total Q1 Budget as $ amounts with comma separators and two decimals (derived from the CSVs), e.g., "$12,345.67".
  - Name the two categories with the largest absolute variances and state whether each is over or under budget (based on your computed results).
  - Close with one sentence noting that the updated chart config enables screen-reader support and a color-contrast-friendly palette.

Deliverables:
- output/q1_category_variance.csv
- Modified src/chartConfig.json (in place)
- output/stakeholder_update.md

Assumptions/constraints:
- Treat dates as ISO strings and consider only 2024-01 through 2024-03 for Q1.
- No new files besides those under output/, and do not rename the provided inputs.
- Do not use external services; everything should be computed locally from the provided files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
